examples.title: Verify Python Installation
examples.description: Basic verification script to confirm Python and pip are correctly installed with required version
examples.code:
BEGIN
 IMPORT sys, subprocess
 
 // Check Python version
 PRINT "Python Version: " + sys.version
 
 // Verify minimum version (Python 3.6+ required for modern IoT)
 IF sys.version_info.major >= 3 AND sys.version_info.minor >= 6 THEN
 PRINT "✓ Python version is compatible for IoT development"
 ELSE
 PRINT "✗ Please upgrade to Python 3.6 or higher"
 ENDIF
 
 // Check pip availability
 TRY
 pip_version = subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "--version"], 
 capture_output=True, text=True)
 PRINT "Pip Version: " + pip_version.stdout
 PRINT "✓ Pip is available"
 EXCEPT
 PRINT "✗ Pip not found - reinstall Python with pip"
 ENDTRY
 
 // List installed IoT-relevant packages
 PRINT "\nInstalled packages:"
 list_packages = subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "list"], 
 capture_output=True, text=True)
 PRINT list_packages.stdout
END
examples.explanation: This verification script checks that Python 3.6+ is installed (required for modern IoT libraries), pip is available, and displays all installed packages - essential first step after installation.

examples.title: Setup IoT Virtual Environment
examples.description: Script to create and configure a virtual environment for IoT projects with all necessary packages
examples.code:
BEGIN
 IMPORT os, subprocess, sys
 
 // Step 1: Create virtual environment
 project_name = "IoT_Weather_Project"
 venv_path = "./" + project_name + "_env"
 
 PRINT "Creating virtual environment: " + venv_path
 subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "venv", venv_path])
 
 // Step 2: Determine activate script path (OS-dependent)
 IF os.name == "nt" THEN
 activate_path = venv_path + "\\Scripts\\activate"
 ELSE
 activate_path = venv_path + "/bin/activate"
 ENDIF
 
 // Step 3: Install required IoT packages
 iot_packages = [
 "RPi.GPIO", // GPIO control for Raspberry Pi
 "Adafruit_DHT", // Temperature/humidity sensors
 "spidev", // SPI communication
 "requests", // HTTP for API calls
 "flask" // Web framework for IoT dashboard
 ]
 
 PRINT "Installing IoT packages..."
 FOR package IN iot_packages DO
 subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", package])
 PRINT "✓ Installed: " + package
 ENDFOR
 
 // Step 4: Generate requirements.txt
 freeze_output = subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "freeze"],
 capture_output=True, text=True)
 
 file = OPEN("requirements.txt", "w")
 WRITE(file, freeze_output.stdout)
 CLOSE(file)
 
 PRINT "✓ Virtual environment ready!"
 PRINT "✓ Requirements saved to requirements.txt"
 PRINT "To activate: source " + activate_path
END
examples.explanation: Automates the creation of an isolated Python environment with essential IoT packages, ensuring reproducible project setups across different machines.

examples.title: Check and Install IoT Dependencies
examples.description: Script to check if required IoT packages are installed and install missing ones
examples.code:
BEGIN
 IMPORT subprocess, sys
 
 // Required packages for IoT project
 REQUIRED_PACKAGES = [
 "RPi.GPIO", 
 "Adafruit_DHT", 
 "requests", 
 "spidev"
 ]
 
 // Function to check if package is installed
 FUNCTION is_installed(package_name)
 result = subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "show", package_name],
 capture_output=True, text=True)
 RETURN result.returncode == 0
 ENDFUNCTION
 
 // Main execution
 PRINT "Checking IoT dependencies..."
 missing_packages = []
 
 FOR package IN REQUIRED_PACKAGES DO
 IF is_installed(package) THEN
 PRINT "✓ " + package + " is installed"
 ELSE
 PRINT "✗ " + package + " is missing"
 ADD package TO missing_packages
 ENDIF
 ENDFOR
 
 // Install missing packages
 IF LENGTH(missing_packages) > 0 THEN
 PRINT "\nInstalling missing packages..."
 FOR package IN missing_packages DO
 subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", package])
 PRINT "✓ Installed: " + package
 ENDFOR
 ELSE
 PRINT "\n✓ All IoT dependencies are satisfied!"
 ENDIF
 
 // Try importing IoT modules
 PRINT "\nVerifying imports..."
 TRY
 IMPORT RPi.GPIO
 PRINT "✓ RPi.GPIO imported successfully"
 EXCEPT ImportError
 PRINT "✗ RPi.GPIO import failed (not on Raspberry Pi?)"
 ENDTRY
END
examples.explanation: Ensures all required IoT packages are present before running an IoT project. Handles both installation and graceful handling when running on non-Raspberry Pi systems.
